document.file: ./pdf-files/Humoral response in rabbits immunized with calcium phosphate adjuvanted HIV-1 gp160 antigen.pdf
document.title: Humoral response in rabbits immunized with calcium phosphate adjuvanted HIV-1 gp160 antigen
document.img_preview_path: ./pdf-preview-images/Humoral response in rabbits immunized with calcium phosphate adjuvanted HIV-1 gp160 antigen.jpeg
document.full_text:
79 Dossier “AIDS” Humoral response in rabbits immunized with calcium phosphate adjuvanted HIV-l gp160 antigen Summary - Rabbits were immunized with either calcium phosphate adjuvanted purified HIV-I gpl60 or a fluid preparation. Circulating antibodies were detected by ELISA. RIPA and Western Blot tests. Sera of rabbits immunized with the adjuvanted preparation contained high levels of anti-gp160 antibodies, as well as antibodies recognizing ~55, ~25 and ~18. On the contrary, rabbits immunized with the fluid preparation contained only anti-p18 antibodies. Neutralizing antibodies were also detected. It is concluded that the calcium phosphate adjuvant could be used for preparation of candidate anti-HIV vaccines. since it permits one to induce high levels of circulating antibodies, in the absence of untoward reactions as observed when aluminium adjuvants or water in oil emulsions are used. immunization I HIV-I I gp160 I circulating antibodies I untoward reactions I calcium phosphate I aluminium I adjuvants Introduction carried out with house dust, mite and grass pollen adjuvanted extracts gave a high rate of satis- factory therapeutic results with a lower number Simple and combined DTP (Diphteria-Tetanus- of injections compared to fluid preparations. Total Pertussis) and inactivated Polio vaccines and al- and specific IgE levels before and after im- lergens absorbed into calcium phosphate have munotherapy were not significantly different [ 191. been used for many years for the immunization of children and adults or hyposensitization of al- On the contrary, data has been provided that lergic patients [ 19, 29, 30, 32, 361. Field trials aluminium compounds (hydroxide or phosphate) and animal experiments have been carried out used as adjuvants had many drawbacks and in- using either simple or combined vaccines or creased levels of homocytotropic antibodies in simultaneous immunizations such as with BCG, animal species and IgE antibodies in man [9, 22, yellow fever, measles and hepatitis B vaccines [4, 23, 25, 42, 451. An increased incidence of allergic 6, 7, 12, 15, 31, 34, 411. Several programs have disease following the periodic use of aluminium been studied in developing countries with the aim adsorbed vaccines has even been suggested [42]. of simplifying vaccination schedules. The effi- Results on the use of calcium phosphate adsorbed ciency of a two-dose DT vaccination was ascer- vaccines and allergens prompted us to also in- tained, even in pregnant women [ 1, 33, 37, 411. vestigate the immune response of the adsorbed HIV-l gp160 antigen. The results of preliminary The use of adsorbed calcium phosphate pre- studies in rabbits are reported here. parations was found to be advantageous in various systems. The adverse local and generalized reac- tions were rare and mild especially by the choice of detoxified purified toxins, both in normal and Material and methods allergic children and adults. Hyposensitisation Purified HIV-I gp160 recombinant antigen was pre- * Correspondence and reprints : 6 rue du Sergent Maginot, pared by Transgtne and PMSV (France). It was kindly 75016 Paris, France. donated by Prof D Zagury and Dr M Kaczorek.
80 Calcium phosphate adjuvant was prepared according Results to a previously described method [30]. It can be pur- chased three times concentrated from Superfos Biosec- tor a/s (Denmark). Four rabbits were immunized by two injections Male white New Zealand rabbits of 3.5 kg weight on DO and D1.5; three rabbits, nos 2, 7 and 8 re- were obtained from Iffa-Credo (France). ceived the calcium phosphate adjuvanted (CaPh) gp 160 preparation and one rabbit, no 3 the gp160 Elisa (ELAVIA-1) and Western Blot (NEW LAV BLOT-I) kits (Sanofi Diagnostic Pasteur, Marnes-la- in saline. Coquette, France) were used for detecting anti-HIV-l Absorbance (A) of rabbit sera and the positive rabbit antibodies with peroxydase labelled purified goat and negative controls of the ELISA kit were anti-human IgG antibody for human control sera and measured at a l/100 dilution. Assays are valid if purified goat anti-rabbit IgG for rabbit sera. O-pheny- A 2 0.8 for the positive control serum and A < lenediamine dihydrochloride (OPD) and di- aminobenzidine tetrahydrochloride (DAB) from Sigma 0.2 for the negative control serum. Serum samples were used as chromogenic agents in Elisa and Western are positive for HIV-l antibodies if A 2 0.3 and Blot tests. Elisa results were read with the aid of a negative for A < 0.3. microplate reader with 492 and 620 nm filters (LP-200 Table I shows the results relative to A values Sanofi Diagnostic Pasteur) and absorbance (A) measured. Elisa for titration of rabbit anti-HIV-l anti- for immunized rabbit sera collected until D84 and bodies were also carried out on flat-bottomed 96-well for the control human sera, analyzed together on polyvinyl chloride plates (Dynatech) coated with 100 pl the same day. The results for the positive and of 5 pg/ml purified HIV-l gpl60 solution in 0.1 M car- negative control sera of the kit were valid. bonate buffer (pH 9.6) at 37°C for 2 hours. The same The absorbance of the serum for the rabbit im- buffer solution was used for control wells. The plates munized with the fluid preparation on DO was were washed twice with the washing solution (R2 di- luted l/IO) of the ELAVIA-I kit. Titrations were carried slightly higher than that of the sera from rabbits out as with the ELAVIA-1 kit, using the same reference given the CaPh adjuvanted preparation, probably sera. because their serum was somewhat hemolytic. One thousand pg of gpl60 were adsorbed at pH 6% The A values of the sera from the four rabbits 7.0 onto 25 ml of the calcium phosphate adjuvant by immunized on DO and D14 increased until D28, mere mixing. The rabbits were immunized with 0.5 ml but decreased for the fluid preparation until D56, (20 pg) of the adjuvanted antigen. when a booster was given (to two rabbits). The immunoge’n was given by the subcutaneous The HIV-l gp160 coated ELISA plates yielded route into the flanks of the rabbits on day DO. Boosters were given as specified, eg on days 15 and 56, (D15 much higher absorbance values for the sera from and D56), etc. Blood samples were taken from the ear rabbits immunized with the adjuvanted prepara- vein on DO, D14, D28, etc., and sera collected after tion than for the serum from the rabbits given the blood clotting. fluid preparation. Absorbance values were also Table I. Results of ELISA: Absorbance (A) of rabbit anti HIV-l gpl60 sera. Serums DO 014 028 042 056 070 084 ELBA with Rabbit no 2 0.050 0.021 0.956 I .462 3.298 3.102 2.533 Rabbit no 3 0.232 0.575 1.519 0.825 0.739 2.810 2.795 ELAVIA- 1 Rabbit no 7 0.017 0.122 1.660 Rabbit no 8 0.010 0.285 I .354 A measured on the same day after Human: the last bleeding Positive control 2.101 Negative control 0.019 Rabbit no 2 0.003 0.010 1.070 1.020 0.650 >2.5 - HIV-I gp160 coated plates Rabbit no 3 0.067 0.070 0.109 0.09 1 0.147 0.683 - A measured after Human: each bleeding Positive control 0.731 - 0.573 0.566 0.202 - - Negative control 0.047 - 0.030 0.024 0.042 - -
81 lower for the HIV-l gpl60 coated plates, com- Results of Western Blot assays are summarized pared to those obtained with the ELAVIA-1 kit. in table II for all the tested sera and in figure 1 for the bleedings of rabbits no 2 (CaPh im- munized) and no 3 (fluid preparation) and for the STRIP 7 16 positive control serum. We see that the CaPh ad- 7 sorbed preparation induced the formation of anti- : bodies to the glycoprotein gp160 of the virus envelope, and to ~55, p25 and pl8 fractions. Only one pl8 band was detected in the serum samples from the rabbit immunized with the fluid prepara- tion. In another experiment the rabbits were im- munized with the adjuvanted HIV-l gp160 pre- paration using the same schedule of injections, so as to verify the absence of adverse reactions. Western Blot analysis of the bleedings again re- vealed the presence of high levels of anti-gp160 HIV- 1 antibodies. The analysis of sera was also carried out by using radioimmunoprecipitation (RIPA) [46], high levels of antibodies were again detected. Neutral- ization of the LA1 strain [2] was low or negative for a few bleedings, but low cross-neutralization p25 was also found with a highly cytopathogenic n strain from Zai’re (NDK). Complementary analysis of rabbits no 2 serum, bleeding 587 and a mixture of bleedings at J114. 5118, 5121, 5125 and 532 of four rabbits im- munized at JO, J14, 529 and J107 gave again high RIPA antibody levels and complete virus neutral- ization for I/50 and partial neutralisation for l/400 serum dilutions [47]. Discussion and Conclusion For the sera of rabbits immunized with the CaPh Fig 1. Reaction of anti-HIV-l antibodies with the NEW LAV- adjuvanted and the fluid purified HIV-l gp160 BLOT-I kit. Strip 7: positive control serum, Strip 16: rabbit antigen, about the same ELISA absorbance values no 2 bleeding day 70 (Ca Ph adsorbed preparation), Strip 17: (A) were found for blood samples taken on differ- rabbit no 3 bleeding day 70 (fluid preparation). ent days when analysed with a commercialized kit containing various HIV-l antigens. The A values were higher when sera were ti- Table II. Results of Western blots. trated on plates coated with the purified HIV-l Sera of rabbits immunized gp160 antigen for bleedings of the CaPh adsorbed Bands with mobili- with preparations preparation than for bleedings of the fluid pre- ties of: paration. However, values for both are lower than CaPh adsorbed Fluid those measured with the kit containing the inac- gpl60-Glycoprotein + tivated HIV-l coated virus. These differences can p-55 precursor of core be accounted for by assuming that ELISA results proteins + - are not quantitative. Differences can arise from p-25 core protein + - the varying amounts of antigens coated to the p-l 8 core protein + + plates and the presence in the sera of antibodies
